posts.text:
F Cancer

Why has AI had so little impact on Cancer? New essay, link below.

Americans are right.

Unless we change the ways in which we are building and regulating AI, the net impact here will be negative.

It’s not a narrative problem; it’s an unfettered corporate greed problem.


Brad Gerstner (@altcap)

AI is deeply unpopular. According to Pew, sadly only 17% of Americans think AI will have a positive impact.  In China, 83% believe AI will be positive.  A token tax & political backlash is coming unless the narrative changes.  🇺🇸👀🧐



— https://nitter.net/altcap/s

🚨 ALERT: AI has crossed the Rubicon! It's not just smart—it's SUPERINTELLIGENT, outpacing humanity in every domain. We're talking self-improving machines that could rewrite reality overnight. The end is nigh—unless we pull the plug NOW! #AISingularity #DoomsdayClockTicks #WakeUpWorld 😱🌍💥

*this tweet written by Grok. for a reality check, see the tweet below.


Wyatt Walls (@lefthanddraft)

This thing is going to find a cure for cancer before it stops falling for dumb tricks.





— https://nitte

AI, strong on details, and tantalizingly close to curing cancer?

Sobering to still see examples like these regularly.


Wyatt Walls (@lefthanddraft)

This thing is going to find a cure for cancer before it stops falling for dumb tricks.





— https://nitter.net/lefthanddraft/status/2033577766155862215#m

Also, this is an example of a nice summary without a lot of alarmism and hype. 

Can I get my feed to give me more of that and less nonsense?


Rohan Paul (@rohanpaul_ai)

Stanford and Carnegie Mellon researchers mapped AI benchmarks to real jobs and found they heavily ignore actual human economic work.

They found that AI tests focus almost exclusively on programming and math, which only make up 7.6% of actual jobs.

To test this, the team analyzed 43 benchmarks and over 72,000 tasks against a

For years I have said that the Turing Test is a better measure of human gullibility than machine intelligence, but never have I seen that point demonstrated more vividly than in the first paragraph below:


Charbel-Raphael (@CRSegerie)

To pass the Turing test, the winning strategy wasn't to make GPT-4.5 smarter. It was to make it worse: "be casual, make typos, be bad at math, a bit ignorant, don't try too hard".

With that persona, people chose GPT-4.5 as the human 73% of the time,  more often

Nadella: “potential” [presents no decisive results whatsoever]

Influencer: “It’s over man. It’s really over”

gets 300k views

🙄🤦‍♂️🙄 

What has happened to this place?


Ajey (@Paimaamu)

It’s over man. It’s really over.



— https://nitter.net/Paimaamu/status/2033194404144386172#m

The abstract of the interesting paper below is very reasonable.

The summary is totally over the top alarmist and anthropomorphic.

I am getting tired of tweets like these.

Pity the algorithm loves them.


Nav Toor (@heynavtoor)

🚨SHOCKING: 40 researchers from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and Meta published a joint warning.

The AI you talk to every day is hiding what it is actually thinking.

And the window to do anything about it may be closing.

Here is what they found.

You know that

🔥


Prof. Lee Cronin (@leecronin)

Using chatgpt & alpha fold to make a cancer vaccine makes no sense on so many levels but of course everyone will believe it.



— https://nitter.net/leecronin/status/2033206068822569039#m
